title: Validation System
description: Pluggable async validation framework: register built-in and custom rules, define named aliases (email, phone), integrate translated messages, and validate programmatically or via ax-validation-rule in forms.
imports: import { AXValidationService, AXValidationRegistryService, AXValidationModule, AX_VALIDATION_CONFIG, validationConfig, AXValidationRule, AXValidationRuleResult, AXValidationSummary, AXRequiredValidationRule, AXRegexValidationRule, AXCallbackValidationRule } from '@acorex/core/validation';

hints.title: Bootstrap with forRoot
hints.content: Call AXValidationModule.forRoot() in app.config.ts via importProvidersFrom. This registers all built-in rules into AXValidationRegistryService.

hints.title: Translated messages
hints.content: Pass translation keys as message (e.g. @acorex:validation.messages.required). The service auto-translates via AXTranslationService.

hints.title: Named aliases
hints.content: Use validationConfig() to map short names like email and phone to underlying rules with preset options.

hints.title: Form integration
hints.content: In templates, attach rules with <ax-validation-rule rule="required"> inside input components. Validate the whole form with await form.validate().

methods.content:
/**
 * Validates value against a rule name or config alias.
 * Translates options.message when provided.
 */
validate(ruleName: string, value: unknown, options?: AXValidationRuleOptions): Promise<AXValidationRuleResult>

/**
 * Fluent chain — add multiple rules then call validate().
 */
ruleFor(value: unknown): AXValidationRuleChain

types.title: AXValidationRule
types.description: Abstract base class for custom rules.

methods.content: abstract validate(value: unknown, options?: AXValidationRuleOptions): Promise<AXValidationRuleResult>

types.title: AXValidationRuleResult
types.description: Result of a single rule execution.

types.title: AXValidationSummary
types.description: Aggregated result from a rule chain.

methods.content: required — non-empty string, non-null, non-empty array, valid number
callback — custom validate function (sync, async, or boolean)
minLength / maxLength / length — string length constraints
equal — lodash isEqual, optional not flag
greaterThan / lessThan — numeric compare, optional inclusive
between — range check for number or Date

types.title: AX_VALIDATION_CONFIG
types.description: InjectionToken for named rule aliases. Default includes email and phone.

content.data:
@acorex/core/validation provides a rule-based validation engine used by ACoreX form inputs and services.

Architecture:
1. AXValidationModule.forRoot() registers built-in rule classes
2. AXValidationRegistryService stores rule instances by name
3. AXValidationService.validate() runs a rule (or resolves a config alias like email)
4. AXValuableComponent (CDK) collects rules from ax-validation-rule directives and runs ruleFor().validate()

All rules return Promise<AXValidationRuleResult>. Messages support translation keys and {{param}} interpolation via AXTranslationService.

Default config aliases:
• email → regex with email pattern
• phone → regex with phone pattern

sections.title: Basic Usage
sections.description: Register AXValidationModule.forRoot() once, then inject AXValidationService to validate values programmatically.

sections.title: App Setup
sections.description: Register the module and optional custom config.

sections.title: Single Rule Validation
sections.description: Validate programmatically from a service or component.

sections.title: Fluent Rule Chain
sections.description: Run multiple rules against one value.

sections.title: Related packages (not exported here)
sections.description: Template forms and ax-validation-rule live elsewhere.

content.data:
The following are **not** part of @acorex/core/validation and must not be imported from this package:

• AXFormModule, AXFormComponent → @acorex/components/form
• ax-validation-rule directive → @acorex/components/form
• ax-form, ax-text-box, ax-number-box → @acorex/components/*

Those components call AXValidationService internally (via AXValuableComponent in @acorex/cdk/common). When you need declarative rules in HTML or form.validate(), read the @acorex/components/form documentation instead.

This package only documents programmatic validation:

await validation.validate('email', value);
await validation.ruleFor(value).addRule('required').validate();

sections.title: Callback Rule
sections.description: Custom validation logic via AXValidationService — no form modules required.

sections.title: Custom Validation Rule
sections.description: Create and register a new rule class.

content.data:
| Rule | Options | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| required | message?, title? | Fails on null, empty string, empty array, NaN, false boolean |
| regex | pattern (string \| RegExp), message?, title? | Skips empty values (passes) |
| callback | validate (fn), message? | fn returns boolean, { result, message }, or Promise |
| minLength | value (number), message? | Skips empty values |
| maxLength | value (number), message? | Skips empty values |
| length | min, max, message? | Exact range |
| equal | value, not?, message? | Uses lodash isEqual |
| greaterThan | value, inclusive?, message? | Skips null/undefined |
| lessThan | value, inclusive?, message? | Skips null/undefined |
| between | lowerValue, upperValue, inclusive?, message? | number or Date |
| email | (alias) | Maps to regex with default email pattern |
| phone | (alias) | Maps to regex with default phone pattern |

Rule names are case-sensitive: use minLength and maxLength (camelCase), not minlength.

sections.title: Translation Messages
sections.description: Default i18n keys in acorex scope.

sections.title: How Form Components Consume Validation
sections.description: Cross-package flow — documented for context only.

content.data:
This section describes how other ACoreX packages use the validation engine. None of the items below are exported from @acorex/core/validation.

1. AXValidationModule.forRoot() registers rules (this package)
2. AXValidationService.validate() runs a rule (this package)
3. @acorex/components/form provides ax-validation-rule directive
4. @acorex/cdk/common AXValuableComponent calls validationService.ruleFor(value) internally
5. @acorex/components/form AXFormComponent orchestrates field validation on submit

For template-based forms, see @acorex/components/form documentation. For programmatic checks, use AXValidationService directly as shown above.

| Need | Use (this package) |
|------|-----|
| Bootstrap rules | AXValidationModule.forRoot() |
| Named shortcuts (email, phone) | validationConfig() + AX_VALIDATION_CONFIG |
| Single field check | validation.validate(rule, value, options) |
| Multiple rules on one value | validation.ruleFor(value).addRule(...).validate() |
| Custom business logic | callback rule or custom AXValidationRule class |
| Register new rule class | AXValidationModule.forRoot({ rules: [MyRule] }) |

| Need | Use (other packages) |
|------|-----|
| Template declarative rules | ax-validation-rule from @acorex/components/form |
| Whole form validation | AXFormComponent from @acorex/components/form |
